meta.title: Growing more for less
meta.originalTitle: Growing more for less 卫星农业

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on
, which are based on Reading Passage 2 below.
Satellite technology is helping farmers boost crop yields

For farmers, working out the optimal amount of seed, fertiliser, pesticide and water to scatter on a field can make, or break, the subsequent harvest. Regular laboratory analyses of soil and plant samples from various parts of the field can help - but such expertise is costly, and often unavailable. However, a new and cheaper method of doing this analysis is now on offer. Precise prescriptions for growing crops can be obtained quickly, and less expensively, by calculating the amount of electromagnetic radiation reflected from agricultural land. The data is collected by orbiting satellites.

Examining the wavelength of the radiation that is reflected can reveal, with surprising precision, the properties of the soil, the quantity of crop being grown, and the levels in those crops of chlorophyll*, various minerals, moisture and other indicators of their quality. If recent and forecast weather data is added to the mix, detailed maps can be produced indicating exactly how, where and when crops should be grown. The service usually costs less than US$15 per hectare for a handful of readings a year, and can increase yields by as much as 10%.

Such precision farming using satellite-based intelligence is a relatively new technique. Even so, it is catching on quickly. Five times a year, for example, a French cereals growers' co-operative called Sevepi purchases satellite data and makes it available to its members in the form of maps of their fields, divided into three or four colour-coded zones per hectare. For each zone, one of about 50 fertiliser formulas is recommended. On top of this, if the wheat in the field has already grown quite high early in the season, and heavy showers are expected, an appropriate dose of growth regulator is recommended for each zone. (Long, fragile stems break more easily in downpours.) Then, farm vehicles equipped with global-positioning system locators automatically mix and apply the prescribed dose to each area.

France is the pioneer in this sort of surveillance. More farmland is analysed by satellite there than in any other country, according to Infoterra (a subsidiary of EADS Astrium), the firm that is France's largest provider of such information, supplying data to companies such as Sevepi. Moreover, Henri Douche, head of Infoterra's agriculture sales in Toulouse, reckons the amount of monitored farmland will increase as weather patterns change and farmers can no longer rely on the past as a guide to the future. When confounded by the yield variations that these new weather patterns will bring, even farmers who are afraid of new technology will sign up, he says.

Inexpensive data on the productivity of land is advantageous to governments, too. Areas where fertilisers and pesticides are being applied excessively can be pinpointed, studied and regulated by environmental and land-use agencies. Guy Lafond, an agronomist with Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada, a government agency, says the satellite data it purchases is proving useful for the study of fields with declining productivity in the province of Saskatchewan. Overkill with nitrate fertilisers (which are also a source of greenhouse gases) appears partly responsible. And according to RapidEye, a German satellite operator, insurance companies are also studying satellite data with a view to selling insurance policies to governments of famine-prone countries that might be threatened by crop failure.

In March, RapidEye began selling data that helps forecast harvests. 'Too often, farmers limit productivity by managing fields uniformly,' says Fredrick Jung-Rothenhausler, head of product development at the firm's headquarters in Brandenburg an der Havel. 'Our satellites are the first commercial satellites to include the Red-Edge band of the light spectrum, which is sensitive to changes in chlorophyll content. More research will be necessary to realise the full potential of the Red-Edge band. However, this band can assist in monitoring vegetation health, improving species separation and also help in measuring protein and nitrogen content in biomass.' The company's data, which comes from both Europe and the Americas, breaks field productivity down into patches just five metres square.

The advantages that satellite technology provides in terms of precision farming do not have to be restricted to rich countries. In Africa, where many soils have become badly depleted of nutrients, better fertiliser management would greatly improve the situation. As a consequence, the charitable trust World Agroforestry Centre, in the city of Nairobi, in Kenya, has begun to build up a catalogue of the radiation patterns derived from around 100,000 samples of African soils. The information is the key to understanding the potential of these soils to be more agriculturally productive. Once passed on to the International Centre for Tropical Agriculture, based in Colombia, South America, it is intended that the information be used to build a database called the 'Digital Soil Map'. When ready, this will provide farmers with free forecasts, developed with regularly updated satellite imagery, across farmland in a number of countries in Africa. This is information which will almost certainly assist in improving crop yields. For a hunger-ravaged continent, that is good news indeed.
* chlorophyll: the green substance in plants that absorbs light from the sun
Compiled, formatted, and lightly proofread by ZYZ Reading Walks. All copyright in the underlying works remains with the original authors and publishers.
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Write the correct letter, A-G, in boxes 14-20 on your answer sheet.
You may use any letter more than once.
an example of how farmers in one country are now using satellite data to determine fertiliser use
a reference to climate change and its effects
a reference to the effect on the soil of using too much fertiliser
an example of information that will be shared between different countries
a mention of the country which is the leader in agricultural technology
a description of an innovation in satellite imaging which requires further study
evidence of the cost effectiveness of using satellite technology in agriculture

explanationSection.text:
题目 14
答案：C
PDF 定位：farmers in one country → a French.. using satelite data +→ purchases satelite data / satelitebased intelligence; determine fertiliser use → fertiser formulas is recommended for each zone
PDF 解析：C段:*a French cerealgrowers'co 题干要"例子+施肥决策"。C段给出完整流程： ..Sevepi购买卫星数据...,每个分区推荐约 买卫星数据→分区地图→每区推荐肥料配方。B operative called Sevepi purchases satllite 50种肥料配方中的一种.* 段也提到'maps'但没有到"施肥方案"层面，因 data... For each zone, one of about 50 此不选B。 fertiliser formulas is recommended.

题目 15
答案：D
PDF 定位：climate change (不定直呼)→ weather effects +→ yield variations / can no longer ased up uo Ajay
PDF 解析：D: *..monitored farmland will ncrease as 天气模式变化不能再依蒙过去.新天 题干是"气候变化及影响"。这里用"weather patterns change"表达"气候/天气规律改变"， weather patterns change and farmers can 气模式带来产量波动 Pak, + ed a uo Aa Jabuoou 并给出影响：过去经验失效+产量波动。 variations..new weather patterns..*

题目 16
答案：E
PDF 定位：too much fertiliser → overkil with nitrate fertilisers; effect on soil/land → fields with declining productivity (地力/产出下降）
PDF 解析：ER: .fields with declining productivity.. 田地生产力下障.酸盐肥料过量似乎 这里不是直接说"soildamaged"。需是用结果 部分负责。" *declining productivity体现土地/土境层面的 Overkill with nitratefertilisers.. appears 负面影响。 陷阱：B酸提~minerals/moisture partly responsible.* 但只是检测指标，不是"过量施肥的后果"。

题目 17
答案：G
PDF 定位：shared between different countries → passed on to.based in Colombia (跨国传 遇) + across farmland in a number of countries in Africa (多国使用）
PDF 解析：题干要"跨国共享"的例子：非洲→哥伦比亚→ GIR: "Once passed on to the International "传给位于哥伦比亚的机构再用于非洲多个国 家的农田。" 再服务非洲多国，非常清楚。 Centre for Tropical Agriculture, based in Colombia. provide across farmland in a number of countries in Africa.*

题目 18
答案：D
PDF 定位：leader ploneer: leader in... more.. than any other country
PDF 解析：题干"leader"在文中被替换成"pioneer+more D: *France is the pioneer.. More 分析农田多于任何国家 "法国是先驱.. than any other country". farmland.. than in any other country..

题目 19
答案：F
PDF 定位：innovation in satllite imaging → Red-Edge band. sensitive to changes in chlorophyl: requires further study → More research wil
PDF 解析：".-加入红边波段 两段式匹配：先"新东西是什么"，再"还需研 FR: *include the RedEdge band_. More ..要充分发挥潜力仍需更 究"。这题几乎是送分。 research willbe necessary to realise the 多研究。" full potential.

题目 20
答案：B
PDF 定位：be necessary cost effectiveness costs less than..+
PDF 解析：题干要"证据"，所以必须找成本+收益的量化。 B段: ..costs less than US$15 per "每公项不到15美元..产量可提高10%，"

children.text: Write the correct letters in boxes 21 and 22 on your answer sheet.
companies obtain information directly from satellites?

explanationSection.text:
题目 21
答案：B
PDF 定位：increase yields; evidence →具体数字 (<US$15 / +10%)
PDF 解析：B段同时满足， hectare... can increase yields... 10%.*

题目 22
答案：D
PDF 定位：directly → (语境) analysed by satelite; obtain information → provider of such information（提供这种卫星信息）
PDF 解析：D段: "..according to Infoterra. France's .nfotera..是法国此类信息的最大提 为什么选B而不是E?（关键区分)：题目间 largest provider of such information.. "companies obtain information directly from satellites"。文章中只有两费最像"源头方"：① RapidEye被明确纠做satellite operator (源 头方无争议)。②Infoterra出现在"farmland isanalysedbysatellite"的旬子里，并被移为 "provider of such information'（提供这种卫 量监测信息)，语义上就是"卫星信息供应侧"。 再排除剩余：ASevepi:购买数据 (purchases）→不直接，C加拿大机构：购买数 据（purchases）→不直接。EWAC：是慧普机 构/研究机构，核心在"土编样本+目录+数据 库"，并未被写成"卫星运营/直接取数方"； "satelite imagery"在文中是未来会用来更新预 测的来源，但WAC不是量"直接从卫星获取信息的 公司"，

Initially, orbiting satellites are used to measure
believes that confusion about irregular weather will cause more farmers to use satellite technology.
As a result of satellite technology, it may become possible to insure against the risk of
In Africa, much of the soil suffers from the loss of

explanationSection.text:
题目 23
答案：electromagnetic radiation
PDF 定位：起初，地卫星用于测量来自衣田的 measure.. coming from farmland→ calculate the amount of.. refiected from agriufturaland；"comingfrom"在文中体理 为"从农田反射出来"
PDF 解析：计算从农用反射的电磁辐时量数据由缆 A:".calculating the amount of 答累必须直接取：electromagnetic 地卫星收集，" electromagnetic radiation reflected from radiation（两调）最稳， agicultural land. The data ls cllected by orbiting satelites.

题目 24
答案：Henri Douche
PDF 定位：以为不规律天气带来的图意合让更多农民 uosnjuostes/uoga seaggeq confounded; irregular weather → new weather patterns / weather patterns change
PDF 解析：DHenri Douchereckons increase ..HeniDouche 认为..随着天气模式变 人名两阀，特合限制。 化.当农民被新天气模式带案的波动弄得 Jeyeam mau eseuys "kq papunojuo patterns..*

题目 25
答案：crop failure
PDF 定位：可能可以为的风险投保 insure against the risk of insurance policiesthreatened by.; 风险对象→ crop faiure
PDF 解析：直线填cropfailure（两调）。 Ensurance policies.. might be ...可能受到作物股收作物失败质脉. threatened by crop failure.

题目 26
答案：nutrients
